title: Rework quality scaling to preserve full-screen while reducing GPU cost
overview: Replace the single-lever resolutionScale approach with a multi-lever quality system that reduces GPU cost via pixel ratio, point count, AND shader complexity — while guaranteeing the canvas CSS always fills the viewport. The current code already uses pixelRatio scaling (not canvas shrinking), but shader complexity is not tiered and there are no guards or tests proving full-screen is preserved across all quality tiers.

current_state.quality_system: 3-tier (low/medium/high) in src/visual/quality.ts, driven by DPR/cores/memory/screen/touch signals
scaling_levers: enableSparkle boolean (disables treble-modulated lightness on low tier)
missing: No shader complexity tiers — all tiers run full 3-octave noise, pointer repulsion, slow modulation
No dedicated test proving canvas CSS dimensions === viewport across all tiers
No GPU context loss handling for mobile crash prevention
enableSparkle only gates a single if-branch in vertex shaders — not a meaningful GPU cost reduction
current_state.canvas_sizing: Already correct: renderer.setSize(viewport) + style.width/height='100%' + CSS canvas{width:100%!important;height:100%!important}. No layout shrinkage bug exists today.

steps.title: Extend QualityProfile with shaderComplexity field
changes: Add `shaderComplexity: 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'` to QualityProfile interface
Low tier: shaderComplexity='low', Medium: 'medium', High: 'high'
Add `noiseOctaves: 1 | 2 | 3` to QualityProfile (low=1, medium=2, high=3)
Add `enablePointerRepulsion: boolean` (false for low, true for medium/high)
Add `enableSlowModulation: boolean` (false for low, true for medium/high)
steps.rationale: The current system only scales point count and pixel ratio. Shader complexity is the third lever the acceptance criteria requires.

steps.title: Add shader complexity uniforms to vertex shaders
changes: Add `uniform int uNoiseOctaves;` (1/2/3 — controls layeredNoise octave count)
Add `uniform float uEnablePointerRepulsion;` (0.0 or 1.0 — gates pointer repulsion block)
Add `uniform float uEnableSlowModulation;` (0.0 or 1.0 — gates time evolution block)
Refactor layeredNoise() to conditionally compute octaves: always octave 1, add octave 2 if uNoiseOctaves>=2, add octave 3 if uNoiseOctaves>=3
Guard pointer repulsion block with `if (uEnablePointerRepulsion > 0.5 && uPointerDisturbance > 0.0)`
Guard slow modulation block with `if (uEnableSlowModulation > 0.5)`
steps.rationale: Reduces ALU operations per vertex on low-tier devices. 1 octave vs 3 octaves cuts sin() calls from 9 to 3 per vertex. Skipping pointer/slow-mod removes additional sin/cos/multiply chains.

steps.title: Wire shader complexity uniforms into geometry systems
changes: Extend PointCloudConfig and RibbonFieldConfig to accept `noiseOctaves`, `enablePointerRepulsion`, `enableSlowModulation`
Add corresponding uniforms to the ShaderMaterial uniform blocks
Set uniform values from config during init
steps.rationale: Connects the quality profile decisions to the GPU shaders.

steps.title: Pass shader complexity config from main.ts
changes: When creating geometry systems, pass quality.noiseOctaves, quality.enablePointerRepulsion, quality.enableSlowModulation to createPointCloud, createRibbonField, createParticleField configs
steps.rationale: Completes the data flow from quality tier to GPU.

steps.title: Add GPU context loss handler for mobile crash prevention
changes: Add webglcontextlost event listener on the canvas element
Call event.preventDefault() to allow context restoration
Add webglcontextrestored event listener that re-applies renderer state (pixel ratio, size, clear color)
Export the cleanup function for these listeners
steps.rationale: Mobile GPUs frequently lose context under memory pressure. Without handling this, the canvas goes black permanently — which looks like a crash.

steps.title: Harden canvas full-screen guarantee in resize handler
changes: After setSize, assert and re-apply style.width='100%' and style.height='100%' (already done, but add a comment explaining why this is critical for US-036)
No functional change needed — current code is correct
steps.rationale: The resize handler already preserves full-screen. This step is about verifying and documenting the guarantee.

steps.title: Write tests for US-036 acceptance criteria

tests.name: QualityProfile includes shaderComplexity for all tiers
tests.assertion: Low tier has shaderComplexity='low', noiseOctaves=1, enablePointerRepulsion=false, enableSlowModulation=false

tests.name: Medium tier has shaderComplexity='medium' with noiseOctaves=2
tests.assertion: Medium tier profile has expected shader complexity values

tests.name: High tier has shaderComplexity='high' with noiseOctaves=3 and all features enabled
tests.assertion: High tier profile enables all shader features

tests.name: Canvas CSS dimensions always fill viewport regardless of quality tier
tests.assertion: For each tier, after initScene with that tier's resolutionScale, renderer.domElement.style.width === '100%' and style.height === '100%'

tests.name: Low-tier renderer backing store is smaller than high-tier
tests.assertion: renderer pixel ratio with low resolutionScale < renderer pixel ratio with high resolutionScale, while CSS size is identical

tests.name: Shader uniforms reflect quality config for point cloud
tests.assertion: createPointCloud with noiseOctaves=1 produces material with uNoiseOctaves=1

tests.name: Shader uniforms reflect quality config for ribbon field
tests.assertion: createRibbonField with enablePointerRepulsion=false produces material with uEnablePointerRepulsion=0.0

tests.name: Low-tier point cloud has <= maxPoints particles
tests.assertion: Already covered by T-025-22 but re-verified with new config shape

tests.name: Visual identity preserved: all tiers produce non-zero point counts
tests.assertion: For low/medium/high, pointCount > 0 and geometry has positions

tests.name: No localStorage or cookie access during quality-scaled rendering
tests.assertion: Privacy check across all tiers with new shader complexity config

steps.title: Update existing tests for new QualityProfile shape
changes: Update T-025-01 through T-025-08 assertions to include new shaderComplexity, noiseOctaves, enablePointerRepulsion, enableSlowModulation fields
Update T-025-22, T-025-23, T-025-24 to pass new config fields when creating geometry systems
steps.rationale: Existing tests will break when QualityProfile shape changes.

steps.title: Verify build and run full test suite
acceptance: All existing tests pass (updated for new profile shape)

acceptance_criteria_mapping.Canvas/renderer still fills the viewport: Steps 6, 7 (T-036-04, T-036-05). Canvas CSS is always 100% — resolutionScale only affects backing store pixel ratio.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Quality scaling affects pixel ratio, poi: Steps 1-4. QualityProfile now drives three levers: resolutionScale→pixelRatio, maxPoints→point count, shaderComplexity→noise octaves + feature gates.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Mobile does not crash on load: Step 5 (context loss handler) + existing low-tier point count caps (150 particles, 200 points). Low tier also skips expensive shader branches.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Visual identity remains recognisable acr: Step 7 (T-036-09). All tiers produce the same geometric structure (shell+interior point cloud, ribbon bands, particle field) — only density, resolution, and shader detail vary.

risks.risk: Shader branching on uniforms may not save GPU cycles on some hardware (branch prediction)
risks.mitigation: Use integer uniforms and early-exit patterns. The primary savings come from reduced noise octaves (fewer sin() calls), not from branching.

risks.risk: Changing QualityProfile interface breaks downstream consumers
risks.mitigation: New fields are additive. Update all call sites in step 4 and all tests in step 8.

out_of_scope: Adaptive quality (runtime FPS-based tier switching) — separate story
New geometry modes or visual primitives
